From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Paladin of Shadows](/w/index.php?title=Paladin_of_Shadows&redirect=no "Paladin of Shadows"))

Published works by John Ringo

This is the complete list of works by [American](/wiki/Americans "Americans") [military science fiction](/wiki/Military_science_fiction "Military science fiction") writer [John Ringo](/wiki/John_Ringo "John Ringo").

## Bibliography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Bibliography")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This article **needs more complete [citations](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [add missing citation information](/wiki/Help:Footnotes "Help:Footnotes") so that sources are clearly identifiable. *(March 2025)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

### Series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Series")]

#### *Black Tide Rising*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Black Tide Rising")]

The series is based on a [zombie apocalypse](/wiki/Zombie_apocalypse "Zombie apocalypse"), but deals with living, near-rabid, infected humans rather than the living dead. The story centers around a family of survivalists (mother, father, and two teenage daughters) who get early warning of the plague; they escape by boat, only to realize that they are the best hope for others stranded at sea.

* *Under a Graveyard Sky* (September 2013; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4516-3919-8](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4516-3919-8 "Special:BookSources/1-4516-3919-8"))
* *To Sail a Darkling Sea* (February 2014; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4767-3621-9](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4767-3621-9 "Special:BookSources/1-4767-3621-9"))
* *Islands of Rage and Hope* (August 2014; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-4767-3662-4](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-4767-3662-4 "Special:BookSources/978-1-4767-3662-4"))
* *Strands of Sorrow* (January 2015; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-4767-3695-2](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-4767-3695-2 "Special:BookSources/978-1-4767-3695-2"))
* *The Valley of Shadows* (November 2018; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-4814-83551](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-4814-83551 "Special:BookSources/978-1-4814-83551")), co-written with Mike Massa
* *River of Night* (July 2019; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-4814-8421-3](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-4814-8421-3 "Special:BookSources/978-1-4814-8421-3")), co-written with Mike Massa

##### Black Tide Rising Anthologies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Black Tide Rising Anthologies")]

1. *Black Tide Rising* (June 2016; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-4767-8151-8](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-4767-8151-8 "Special:BookSources/978-1-4767-8151-8")), anthology co-edited with Gary Poole
2. *Voices Of The Fall* (March 2019; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-9821-2558-5](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-2558-5 "Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-2558-5")), anthology co-edited with Gary Poole
3. *We Shall Rise* (June 2021; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-9821-2558-5](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-2558-5 "Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-2558-5")), anthology co-edited with Gary Poole
4. *United We Stand* (March 2024; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-9821-9326-3](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-9326-3 "Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-9326-3")), anthology co-edited with Gary Poole

##### Black Tide Rising novels written by other authors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Black Tide Rising novels written by other authors")]

* *At the End of the World* (July 2020; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-9821-2469-4](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-2469-4 "Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-2469-4")), by Charles E. Gannon
* *At the End of the Journey* (May 2021; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-9821-2522-6](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-2522-6 "Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-2522-6")), by Charles E. Gannon
* *Mountain of Fire* (September 2024; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-9821-9361-4](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-9361-4 "Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-9361-4")), by Jason Cordova
* *Across an Ocean of Stars* (April 2025; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-6680-7254-7](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-6680-7254-7 "Special:BookSources/978-1-6680-7254-7")), by Robert E. Hampson
* *Perdition's Storm* (November 2025; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-9648-5641-4](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-9648-5641-4 "Special:BookSources/978-1-9648-5641-4")), by Brian Trent

##### The Graphic Novel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: The Graphic Novel")]

* *Black Tide Rising: The Graphic Novel* co-written with Chuck Dixon (June 2023; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-9821-9263-1](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-9263-1 "Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-9263-1"))

#### *The Council Wars*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: The Council Wars")]

Main article: [The Council Wars](/w/index.php?title=The_Council_Wars&action=edit&redlink=1 "The Council Wars (page does not exist)")

1. *There Will Be Dragons* (November 2003; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-7434-7164-4](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-7434-7164-4 "Special:BookSources/0-7434-7164-4"))
2. *Emerald Sea* (July 2004; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-7434-8833-4](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-7434-8833-4 "Special:BookSources/0-7434-8833-4"))
3. *Against the Tide* (February 2005; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-7434-9884-4](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-7434-9884-4 "Special:BookSources/0-7434-9884-4"))
4. *East of the Sun, West of the Moon* (May 2006; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4165-2059-7](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4165-2059-7 "Special:BookSources/1-4165-2059-7"))

#### *Empire of Man*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Empire of Man")]

Main article: [Empire of Man](/wiki/Empire_of_Man "Empire of Man")

Also known as the "**Prince Roger Series**"

Co-written with [David Weber](/wiki/David_Weber "David Weber"), with multiple books still under contract

1. *[March Upcountry](/wiki/March_Upcountry "March Upcountry")* (May 2001; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-671-31985-X](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-671-31985-X "Special:BookSources/0-671-31985-X"))
2. *[March to the Sea](/wiki/March_to_the_Sea_(novel) "March to the Sea (novel)")* (August 2001; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-671-31826-8](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-671-31826-8 "Special:BookSources/0-671-31826-8"))
3. *[March to the Stars](/wiki/March_to_the_Stars "March to the Stars")* (December 2003; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-7434-3562-1](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-7434-3562-1 "Special:BookSources/0-7434-3562-1"))
4. *[We Few](/wiki/We_Few "We Few")* (April 2005; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-7434-9881-X](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-7434-9881-X "Special:BookSources/0-7434-9881-X"))

Omnibus collections

1. *Empire of Man* (February 2014) [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1476736243](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1476736243 "Special:BookSources/1476736243"); collects *[March Upcountry](/wiki/March_Upcountry "March Upcountry")* and *[March to the Sea](/wiki/March_to_the_Sea_(novel) "March to the Sea (novel)")*
2. *Throne of Stars* (August 2014; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-4767-3666-2](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-4767-3666-2 "Special:BookSources/978-1-4767-3666-2"));[[1]](#cite_note-baen.com-1) collects *[March to the Stars](/wiki/March_to_the_Stars "March to the Stars")* and *[We Few](/wiki/We_Few "We Few")*

#### *Last Judgment’s Fire*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Last Judgment’s Fire")]

Co-written with Kacey Ezell and Christopher L. Smith, this is a post-apocalyptic series centered on a young farmer in search of his destiny who tries to prevent a cataclysmic war. It is set thirty years after "giant electrovoric ants and pterodons" came through a rift in space-time, killing millions of people and disrupting nearly all electrical power systems and generation worldwide.

* *Gunpowder & Embers* (January 2020; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-9821-2428-1](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-2428-1 "Special:BookSources/978-1-9821-2428-1"))

#### *Legacy of the Aldenata*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Legacy of the Aldenata")]

Main article: [Legacy of the Aldenata](/wiki/Legacy_of_the_Aldenata "Legacy of the Aldenata")

Also known as the "**Posleen Series**" and "**Posleen War Series**"[[2]](#cite_note-2) after the name of the invading species besetting and successfully conquering much of Earth.

##### Posleen War—Central storyline

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Posleen War—Central storyline")]

* *[A Hymn Before Battle](/wiki/A_Hymn_Before_Battle "A Hymn Before Battle")* (October 2000; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-671-31941-8](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-671-31941-8 "Special:BookSources/0-671-31941-8"))
* *[Gust Front](/wiki/Gust_Front_(novel) "Gust Front (novel)")* (April 2001; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-671-31976-0](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-671-31976-0 "Special:BookSources/0-671-31976-0"))
* *[When the Devil Dances](/wiki/When_the_Devil_Dances "When the Devil Dances")* (April 2002; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-7434-3540-0](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-7434-3540-0 "Special:BookSources/0-7434-3540-0"))
* *[Hell's Faire](/wiki/Hell%27s_Faire "Hell's Faire")* (January 2003; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-7434-3604-0](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-7434-3604-0 "Special:BookSources/0-7434-3604-0"))

##### Hedren War

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Hedren War")]

* *Eye of the Storm* (July 2009); ([ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4391-32739](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4391-32739 "Special:BookSources/1-4391-32739"))

##### Posleen War side stories

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Posleen War side stories")]

* *[Watch on the Rhine](/wiki/Watch_on_the_Rhine_(novel) "Watch on the Rhine (novel)")* (August 2005; with [Tom Kratman](/wiki/Tom_Kratman "Tom Kratman"); [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-7434-9918-2](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-7434-9918-2 "Special:BookSources/0-7434-9918-2"))
* *[Yellow Eyes](/wiki/Yellow_Eyes "Yellow Eyes")* (April 2007; with Tom Kratman; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4165-2103-8](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4165-2103-8 "Special:BookSources/1-4165-2103-8"))
* *[The Tuloriad](/wiki/The_Tuloriad "The Tuloriad")* (October 2009; with Tom Kratman; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-4391-3304-0](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-4391-3304-0 "Special:BookSources/978-1-4391-3304-0"))

##### *Cally's War* spinoff series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Cally's War spinoff series")]

Co-written with Julie Cochrane, this series is more cloak-and-dagger spy-genre fiction, with humans striving to overcome the game rigged by the Darhel race, which has the rest of the galaxy's races in virtual thralldom—except for the Posleen and humans, whom they fear. The Darhel systematically use humans to combat the Posleen, while bleeding the humans whenever and wherever possible by underhanded clandestine acts to weaken the future options of humanity.

1. *[Cally's War](/wiki/Cally%27s_War "Cally's War")* (October 2004; with Julie Cochrane; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-7434-8845-8](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-7434-8845-8 "Special:BookSources/0-7434-8845-8"))
2. *[Sister Time](/wiki/Sister_Time "Sister Time")* (December 2007; with Julie Cochrane; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4165-4232-9](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4165-4232-9 "Special:BookSources/1-4165-4232-9"))
3. *[Honor of the Clan](/wiki/Honor_of_the_Clan "Honor of the Clan")* (January 2009; with Julie Cochrane; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4165-5591-9](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4165-5591-9 "Special:BookSources/1-4165-5591-9"))

##### Spinoff books

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Spinoff books")]

:   This sequel is set about a [millennium](/wiki/Millennium "Millennium") after the other main Posleen Series works.

* *[The Hero](/wiki/The_Hero_(novel) "The Hero (novel)")* (June 2004; with [Michael Z. Williamson](/wiki/Michael_Z._Williamson "Michael Z. Williamson"); [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-7434-8827-X](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-7434-8827-X "Special:BookSources/0-7434-8827-X"))

#### *Monster Hunter Memoirs*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Monster Hunter Memoirs")]

This series was created after reading Monster Hunter Nemesis. Mr. Ringo wrote most of these three books in about a month. After talking to Larry Correia, the pair was able to put the MHI series under an existing contract between Ringo and Corriea.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Series takes place in the [Monster Hunter International](/wiki/Monster_Hunter_International "Monster Hunter International") Universe during the 1980's and follows a hunter named Chad Gardenier.
Co-written with [Larry Correia](/wiki/Larry_Correia "Larry Correia")

1. *Monster Hunter Memoirs: Grunge* (August 2016; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-4767-8149-5](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-4767-8149-5 "Special:BookSources/978-1-4767-8149-5"))
2. *Monster Hunter Memoirs: Sinners* (December 2016; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-4767-8183-9](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-4767-8183-9 "Special:BookSources/978-1-4767-8183-9"))
3. *Monster Hunter Memoirs: Saints* (July 2018; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-4814-8307-0](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-4814-8307-0 "Special:BookSources/978-1-4814-8307-0"))

#### *Paladin of Shadows*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Paladin of Shadows")]

This is a series of contemporary-era [techno-thrillers](/wiki/Techno-thriller "Techno-thriller"), much like [Tom Clancy](/wiki/Tom_Clancy "Tom Clancy")'s works but with less politics and a closer-to-the-ground level and action focus.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Ringo has stated that these novels stemmed from a nagging idea between contracted books. He believed that the concept was too over-the-top and offensive to be of much interest to his usual audience, and so wrote the first book with no intention of publishing it, as a way to get the idea out of his head. However, during interactions with fans, he mentioned the unpublished story and was surprised that the premise was met with enthusiasm.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The *Paladin of Shadows* series contain graphic scenes of rape, bondage, torture, and underage sex, as Ringo's protagonist's anti-terrorism missions butt heads with harsh economic realities of commercial sexual slavery in Eastern Europe and its connection to funding arms for terrorist organizations.

1. *Ghost* (2005; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4165-0905-4](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4165-0905-4 "Special:BookSources/1-4165-0905-4"))
2. *Kildar* (March 2006; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4165-2064-3](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4165-2064-3 "Special:BookSources/1-4165-2064-3"))
3. *Choosers of the Slain* (July 2006; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4165-2070-8](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4165-2070-8 "Special:BookSources/1-4165-2070-8"))
4. *Unto the Breach* (Dec 2006; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4165-0940-2](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4165-0940-2 "Special:BookSources/1-4165-0940-2"))
5. *A Deeper Blue* (2007; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4165-2128-3](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4165-2128-3 "Special:BookSources/1-4165-2128-3"))
6. *Tiger by the Tail* (2013; with Ryan Sear; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4516-3856-6](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4516-3856-6 "Special:BookSources/1-4516-3856-6"))

The central hero, Michael Harmon (A.K.A. Mike Jenkins, A.K.A. Ghost), is a self-proclaimed [sadist](/wiki/Sadomasochism "Sadomasochism"), repressed rapist, former United States Navy [Boatswain's Mate](/wiki/Boatswain%27s_Mate_(US_Navy) "Boatswain's Mate (US Navy)") [1st Class](/wiki/Petty_officer,_first_class "Petty officer, first class"), and [a trainer of US Navy SEALs](/wiki/United_States_Navy_SEAL_selection_and_training "United States Navy SEAL selection and training"). While walking after a class on the campus of the [University of Georgia](/wiki/University_of_Georgia "University of Georgia"), he witnesses a woman being kidnapped. He impulsively follows the kidnapper and rescues several abducted women and earns the gratitude of several nations, a small fortune, and a series of high-level political connections in the process of experiencing a life change. The work is, in fact, three connected anti-terrorism novellas spanning about a year, backstory omitted from the last two, where "Jenkins" takes on a certain [James Bond](/wiki/James_Bond "James Bond")–style sole operator/loose cannon role. The work features scenes involving the interception of two nuclear devices, saving Paris and Washington, D.C., while featuring a travelogue through part of the seamier sides of the Balkans and European parts of the former Soviet Union.

In the second work, Harmon buys an estate in the eastern Europe country of [Georgia](/wiki/Georgia_(country) "Georgia (country)") that has an entailed ancient warrior tribe, called the Keldara, who bestow on him the honorific "The Kildar" (Warlord, Baron, or similar title). The Keldara aid him in reducing tensions in the Caucasus. Again the book shows a life transition, this time from a sole shooter to a politically connected local warlord. In subsequent books the tribe, now being trained up into a superb light company, goes operational and is employed as a deniable black-ops force by the United States for the next several works. By *A Deeper Blue*, some of the Keldaran force has been trained in both [SCUBA](/wiki/Scuba_diving "Scuba diving") and [HALO jumps](/wiki/HALO_jump "HALO jump"), while *Tiger by the Tail* shows the force on an extended training mission in the Pacific, gradually being transformed *en toto* into a force equivalent to U.S. Navy SEAL Teams—but in company strength.

Other major/recurring characters in the *Paladin of Shadows* series include Charles Adams, a retired SEAL [Master Chief](/wiki/Master_chief_petty_officer "Master chief petty officer"); intel specialist and former [USMC](/wiki/United_States_Marine_Corps "United States Marine Corps") Sergeant Patrick Vanner; [United States Army War College](/wiki/United_States_Army_War_College "United States Army War College") graduate Colonel David Nielson, a retired [Special Ops](/wiki/Special_Forces_(United_States_Army) "Special Forces (United States Army)") Civil Affairs Specialist who serves as Mike's de facto Chief of Staff and the only American officer on his senior staff; Captains Kasey Bathlick and Tamara Wilson (call signs Dragon and Valkryie), two Marine Corps helicopter pilots; Captain John Hardesty, a charter pilot for Chatham Aviation in England/former [Royal Air Force](/wiki/Royal_Air_Force "Royal Air Force") major/[squadron leader](/wiki/Squadron_leader "Squadron leader") who frequently flies Mike around the globe; Colonel Bob Pierson, the Office of Strategic Operations Liaison at [the Pentagon](/wiki/The_Pentagon "The Pentagon") who serves as one of Mike's primary contacts within the United States government; 2nd Lieutenant Britney "Bambi" Harder, who was rescued by Mike in *Ghost* and by *A Deeper Blue* is a junior intelligence officer at [United States Special Operations Command](/wiki/United_States_Special_Operations_Command "United States Special Operations Command") Headquarters, [MacDill Air Force Base](/wiki/MacDill_Air_Force_Base "MacDill Air Force Base"), Florida; Anastasia Rakovitch, a [masochistic](/wiki/Sadomasochism "Sadomasochism") 26-year-old Russian-born woman given to the Kildar by an Uzbek sheikh who now serves as his harem manager; Daria Koroleva, the Kildar's personal assistant; Katya "Cottontail" Ivanova, the sociopathic man-hating whore-turned-spy and assassin; and David and Amanda Cliff, the [President](/wiki/President_of_the_United_States "President of the United States") and [First Lady of the United States](/wiki/First_Lady_of_the_United_States "First Lady of the United States").

#### Shadow's Path

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Shadow's Path")]

1. *Not that Kind of Good Guy* (May 2025; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1668072592](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1668072592 "Special:BookSources/978-1668072592"); [sample](https://www.baen.com/Chapters/9781668072592/9781668072592.htm))
2. *Welcome to the Jungle* (with Casey R. Moores; October 2025; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1668072899](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1668072899 "Special:BookSources/978-1668072899"))

#### *Special Circumstances*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Special Circumstances")]

These novels describe the Foundation for Love and Universal Faith (FLUF), an organization whose members include [Druids](/wiki/Druids "Druids"), [Wiccans](/wiki/Wiccans "Wiccans"), [Asatru](/wiki/Asatru "Asatru"), [Buddhists](/wiki/Buddhists "Buddhists"), and other non-traditional religions. They assist the [FBI](/wiki/FBI "FBI") and other law enforcement agencies worldwide in investigating events and crimes involving the supernatural, which is termed 'Special Circumstances' by the FBI.

1. *Princess of Wands* (January 2006; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4165-0923-2](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4165-0923-2 "Special:BookSources/1-4165-0923-2"))
2. *Queen of Wands* (August 2012; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4516-3789-6](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4516-3789-6 "Special:BookSources/1-4516-3789-6"))

#### *Transdimensional Hunter*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Transdimensional Hunter")]

Inspired by *Pokémon Go* during a time when Ringo was struggling with depression.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The story is about youth woman named Lynn Raven who is recruited to play the Transdimensional Hunter game. But there is more to this game than meets the eye. Series is set in near future 2040s [Cedar Rapids, Iowa](/wiki/Cedar_Rapids,_Iowa "Cedar Rapids, Iowa"). Co-written with [Lydia Sherrer](/w/index.php?title=Lydia_Sherrer&action=edit&redlink=1 "Lydia Sherrer (page does not exist)").

1. *Into the Real* (April 2022) [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1982126001](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1982126001 "Special:BookSources/978-1982126001"))
2. *Through the Storm* (November 2023) [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1982193843](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1982193843 "Special:BookSources/978-1982193843"))
3. *Behind the Veil* (August 2025) [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1964856315](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1964856315 "Special:BookSources/978-1964856315"))
4. *Beyond the Rift* (February 2026) [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1964856506](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1964856506 "Special:BookSources/978-1964856506"))

#### *Troy Rising*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Troy Rising")]

The *Troy Rising* series was inspired by [Howard Tayler](/wiki/Howard_Tayler "Howard Tayler")'s webcomic *[Schlock Mercenary](/wiki/Schlock_Mercenary "Schlock Mercenary")* and its universe. It was created with Tayler's approval, but is not considered canon for the webcomic series. The series is set in the early days of human-alien contact, with humans forced to defend the Earth from the alien invasion.

1. *Live Free or Die* (February 2010; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4391-3332-8](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4391-3332-8 "Special:BookSources/1-4391-3332-8")) [sample chapters](http://www.webscription.net/chapters/1439133328/1439133328.htm?blurb)
2. *Citadel* (January 2011; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4391-3400-6](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4391-3400-6 "Special:BookSources/1-4391-3400-6")) [sample chapters](http://www.webscription.net/chapters/A1439134006/A1439134006__c_.htm) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20151001215243/http://www.webscription.net/chapters/A1439134006/A1439134006__c_.htm) 2015-10-01 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine")
3. *The Hot Gate* (May 2011; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4391-3432-4](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4391-3432-4 "Special:BookSources/1-4391-3432-4")) [sample chapters](http://www.webscription.net/chapters/9781439134320/9781439134320.htm)

#### *Voyage of the Space Bubble*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Voyage of the Space Bubble")]

Main article: [Voyage of the Space Bubble](/wiki/Voyage_of_the_Space_Bubble "Voyage of the Space Bubble")

Also called the *Looking Glass* series.

All books titles in the series are phrases taken from the poem "[Jabberwocky](/wiki/Jabberwocky "Jabberwocky")", which is mentioned repeatedly in the later novels.

2. *Vorpal Blade* (September 2007; with [Travis S. Taylor](/wiki/Travis_Taylor_(author) "Travis Taylor (author)"); [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4165-2129-1](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4165-2129-1 "Special:BookSources/1-4165-2129-1"))
3. *Manxome Foe* (February 2008; with [Travis S. Taylor](/wiki/Travis_Taylor_(author) "Travis Taylor (author)"); [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4165-5521-8](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4165-5521-8 "Special:BookSources/1-4165-5521-8"))
4. *Claws that Catch* (November 2008; with [Travis S. Taylor](/wiki/Travis_Taylor_(author) "Travis Taylor (author)"); [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1-4165-5587-0](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1-4165-5587-0 "Special:BookSources/1-4165-5587-0"))

### Substack

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Substack")]

In March of 2023 John Ringo started a Substack page called Ringo's Taven[[6]](#cite_note-6) for his writing. Many of the books are not complete but this is a reference for what has been publish on the substack so far.

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Mike Truesdale stories")]

2. *Where the Moon Meets the Water* (July 2023)[[8]](#cite_note-8)
3. *Welcome to the Jungle* (September 2023)[[9]](#cite_note-9) Note: This was published as a novel in Oct 2025
4. *Surviving the Game* (January 2024)[[10]](#cite_note-10)
5. *The Kraken* (April 2024)[[11]](#cite_note-11)
6. *The Shadow* (June 2024)[[12]](#cite_note-12)
7. *The Creep* (September 2024)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Prequel stories for Mike Truedale; these are short stories

1. "When MS13 Came to East Baltimore" (October 2024)[[14]](#cite_note-14)
2. "Michael and Los Tres Muertos" (October 2024)[[15]](#cite_note-15)
3. "Michael and the Fifth Street Kings Pt 1" (October 2024)[[16]](#cite_note-16)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Ringo_bibliography&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Incomplete stories")]

The following group is from novels that are incomplete, set in Ringo's pre-Substack published works. To date none of these works have completed to the point where they can be published.
